message:
Implement short-circuit lowering for logical compound member-assignment operators

Lower &&=, ||=, and ??= on member expressions (both static and computed
properties) through proper short-circuit branch ops (JumpIfTruthy,
JumpIfFalsyConsume, JumpIfNullish) instead of rejecting them as
unsupported frontier expressions. The generated IR evaluates the object
and computed key exactly once, reads the current property value, branches
conditionally, and writes back the RHS only when the short-circuit
condition is met. Add integration tests covering all three operators
with both static and computed property access patterns, plus lowering
coverage integration tests.

Addresses RGC-307A1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
